title: rubus horripilus
intro: Rubus horripilus, also known as the bristly blackberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family (Rosaceae) that is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Rubus horripilus include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve cardiovascular health, as well as its possible role in protecting against certain types of cancer. Rubus horripilus can be prepared as a herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and is also used in traditional culinary practices in some Mediterranean countries.
